01.AI (Yi).summary: 01.AI focuses on general-purpose bilingual (Chinese-English) LLMs across multiple sizes, with additional specialized offerings in code generation and vision-language understanding. Their portfolio emphasizes breadth through base, chat, long-context, and quantized variants of each core model series.

clusters.label: Yi-1.5 Base Series
clusters.notes: Second-generation Yi base models in 6B, 9B, and 34B sizes; 32K variants extend context window.

clusters.label: Yi-1.5 Chat Series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned chat variants of Yi-1.5 base models; 16K variants extend context window.

clusters.label: Yi-1.0 Base Series
clusters.notes: First-generation Yi base models; 200K variants offer extremely long context windows.

clusters.label: Yi-1.0 Chat Series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned chat variants of Yi-1.0 base models; 4-bit and 8-bit quantized versions available for the 6B and 34B sizes.

clusters.label: Yi-Coder Series
clusters.notes: Code-specialized models in 1.5B and 9B sizes, each with a base and chat (instruction-tuned) variant.

clusters.label: Yi-VL Vision-Language Series
clusters.notes: Vision-language models built on LLaVA architecture, supporting image-text-to-text tasks in 6B and 34B sizes.

AI21 Labs.summary: AI21 Labs focuses exclusively on their proprietary Jamba architecture, a hybrid SSM/Transformer (Mamba + MoE) model family, releasing general-purpose conversational LLMs in multiple sizes and versions, with recent expansion into reasoning-specialized variants and quantized/compressed deployments.

clusters.label: Jamba Large series (1.5–1.7)
clusters.notes: Flagship large-scale Jamba models (~398.6B parameters) using hybrid Mamba+MoE architecture. Includes FP8 quantized variant for efficient deployment. Multilingual support (en, fr, de) noted in earlier versions.

clusters.label: Jamba Mini series (1.5–1.7)
clusters.notes: Smaller Jamba variant (~51.6B parameters) with the same hybrid Mamba+MoE architecture. Includes FP8 quantized variant. Multilingual support in earlier versions.

clusters.label: Jamba2 series
clusters.notes: Next-generation Jamba2 family with Azure deployment support. Includes a compact 3B model and a Mini (~51.6B) variant with FP8 quantized option.

clusters.label: Jamba Reasoning series
clusters.notes: Specialized reasoning-focused Jamba model at 3B scale, with an associated GGUF quantized variant for local inference. Linked to arxiv paper 2507.02782.

clusters.label: Jamba v0.1 (original release)
clusters.notes: The original public Jamba release introducing the hybrid Mamba+MoE architecture, linked to the foundational arxiv paper 2403.19887.

clusters.label: Jamba tiny / dev / experimental
clusters.notes: Very small Jamba models (0.1B–0.3B) used for development, testing, and experimentation. The reward-dev variant suggests exploration of RLHF/reward modeling pipelines.

Anthropic.summary: Anthropic focuses exclusively on general-purpose large language models under the Claude brand, organized into tiered families (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus) that trade off speed/cost against capability, with consistent instruction-tuned chat interfaces across all releases.

clusters.label: Claude 1 / Early Claude
clusters.notes: First-generation Claude models; Instant 1.2 was a faster, lighter variant optimized for lower latency.

clusters.label: Claude 2 Series
clusters.notes: Second-generation models with improved context windows and reduced hallucination; 2.1 added 200K context support.

clusters.label: Claude 3 Haiku
clusters.notes: Fast, cost-efficient tier of the Claude 3 family; 3.5 Haiku offers improved performance while retaining speed.

clusters.label: Claude 3 Sonnet
clusters.notes: Mid-tier Claude 3 models balancing capability and cost; Sonnet 3.5 became a widely adopted high-performance variant.

clusters.label: Claude 3 Opus
clusters.notes: Top-tier Claude 3 model optimized for complex, nuanced tasks requiring deep reasoning.

clusters.label: Claude 3.7 Sonnet
clusters.notes: Introduced extended thinking / hybrid reasoning mode, marking Anthropic's entry into explicit chain-of-thought reasoning models.

clusters.label: Claude 4 Haiku
clusters.notes: Fourth-generation fast/efficient tier model.

clusters.label: Claude 4 Sonnet
clusters.notes: Fourth-generation mid-tier models with strong reasoning and agentic capabilities.

clusters.label: Claude 4 Opus
clusters.notes: Fourth-generation flagship models representing Anthropic's highest capability tier.

Baichuan.summary: Baichuan is a Chinese AI company focused on bilingual (Chinese-English) language models, with a strong and growing emphasis on medical/healthcare specialized LLMs and multimodal (audio, vision, omni) capabilities, alongside their foundational general-purpose LLM series.

clusters.label: Baichuan 7B/13B Base Series
clusters.notes: First and second generation base pretrained models in 7B and 13B sizes, bilingual Chinese-English.

clusters.label: Baichuan 7B/13B Chat/Instruction Series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned chat variants of the first and second generation models, including 4-bit quantized versions.

clusters.label: Baichuan-M1 Medical Series
clusters.notes: First generation medical-specialized LLMs at 14.5B parameters, available in base and instruct variants.

clusters.label: Baichuan-M2 Medical Series
clusters.notes: Second generation medical LLM at 32B, fine-tuned from Qwen2.5-32B, with quantized variants (GPTQ-Int4 and GGUF).

clusters.label: Baichuan-M3 Medical Series
clusters.notes: Third generation medical MoE LLM at 235B parameters, fine-tuned from Qwen3-235B-A22B, with FP8, GPTQ-INT4, and GGUF quantized variants.

clusters.label: BaichuanMed OCR Medical Vision Series
clusters.notes: Medical vision-language models specialized for OCR and document understanding in healthcare contexts, fine-tuned from Qwen2.5-VL at 7B and 72B scales.

clusters.label: Baichuan Audio Series
clusters.notes: Audio-capable multimodal models at ~10.4B parameters, available in base and instruct variants.

clusters.label: Baichuan Omni Series
clusters.notes: Omni-modal models (~11B) supporting multiple modalities including audio and vision, available in base and instruct variants.

Baidu.summary: Baidu's model portfolio centers on the ERNIE 4.5 family, offering a wide range of dense and MoE language and vision-language models across multiple scales, with dual-framework releases (PaddlePaddle and PyTorch) and quantized variants for deployment flexibility. The portfolio also includes the Qianfan series of multimodal and OCR-specialized models.

clusters.label: ERNIE 4.5 0.3B Dense (Base & Instruct)
clusters.notes: Small 0.3B dense ERNIE 4.5 models released in both PaddlePaddle and PyTorch (PT) variants, covering base and instruction-tuned versions. Bilingual (en/zh).

clusters.label: ERNIE 4.5 21B MoE (Base, Instruct & Thinking)
clusters.notes: Mid-scale MoE models (~21B total, ~3B active) in base, instruct, and thinking/reasoning variants, released across both frameworks.

clusters.label: ERNIE 4.5 300B MoE (Base, Instruct & Quantized)
clusters.notes: Flagship large-scale MoE (~300B total, ~47B active) with extensive quantization variants (2-bit, FP8, W4A8C8) and tensor-parallel configurations for efficient deployment.

clusters.label: ERNIE 4.5 VL 28B MoE Vision-Language (Base, Instruct & Thinking)
clusters.notes: Mid-scale MoE vision-language models (~28B total, ~3B active) supporting image-text-to-text tasks, with base, instruct, and thinking variants.

clusters.label: ERNIE 4.5 VL 424B MoE Vision-Language (Base & Instruct)
clusters.notes: Flagship large-scale MoE vision-language models (~424B total, ~47B active) for image-text-to-text tasks, released in both frameworks.

clusters.label: Qianfan Vision-Language Models
clusters.notes: Qianfan-branded vision-language chat models at 3B, 8B, and 70B scales, offering a range of sizes for different deployment needs.

clusters.label: Qianfan OCR
clusters.notes: Specialized OCR and document intelligence model built on InternVL architecture, focused on optical character recognition tasks.

Cohere.summary: Cohere maintains a broad portfolio spanning multilingual general-purpose chat/instruction models (Command and Aya series), multimodal vision models, embedding and reranking accessories, and an ASR model, with a strong emphasis on multilingual coverage and enterprise-grade deployments.

clusters.label: Command R series
clusters.notes: Core enterprise chat/instruction models with multilingual support; includes quantized (4-bit) variants and an Arabic-specialized fine-tune.

clusters.label: Command A series
clusters.notes: Next-generation flagship Command model at 111B parameters.

clusters.label: Command A specialized variants
clusters.notes: Specialized derivatives of Command A: a reasoning-focused variant, a translation-focused variant, and a multimodal vision variant.

clusters.label: Aya multilingual chat models
clusters.notes: Aya series focused on broad multilingual instruction-following and chat capabilities.

clusters.label: Aya 101 (T5-based multilingual)
clusters.notes: T5-based text2text model covering 101 languages; an earlier research-oriented multilingual model distinct from the later Cohere-architecture Aya models.

clusters.label: Aya Vision multimodal models
clusters.notes: Multimodal image-text models in the Aya family, supporting multilingual vision-language tasks.

clusters.label: Tiny Aya small multilingual models
clusters.notes: Compact 3.3B multilingual models with GGUF quantized variants for efficient deployment; base and instruction-tuned variants across elemental naming scheme.

clusters.label: Cohere Embed models
clusters.notes: Text embedding models for semantic search and retrieval; English and multilingual variants with light (smaller) and full versions.

clusters.label: Cohere Rerank models
clusters.notes: Reranking models for RAG and search pipelines; English and multilingual variants across v2 and v3 generations.

clusters.label: Cohere Transcribe ASR
clusters.notes: Automatic speech recognition model supporting multiple languages including Arabic.

DeepSeek.summary: DeepSeek releases a broad portfolio spanning general-purpose LLMs, specialized models for code, math, reasoning, vision, and OCR, as well as unified multimodal generation models, with a strong emphasis on MoE architectures and distilled reasoning variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-LLM series
clusters.notes: First-generation general-purpose DeepSeek LLMs in 7B and 67B sizes, base and chat variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-MoE series
clusters.notes: Early MoE-based general-purpose models at 16B total parameters.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-V2 series
clusters.notes: 235.7B MoE general-purpose models with Lite variants at 15.7B; base and chat variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-V2.5 series
clusters.notes: Updated V2 general-purpose chat models merging code and general capabilities.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-V3 series
clusters.notes: 684.5B MoE flagship general-purpose models; base and instruction-tuned variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-V3.1 series
clusters.notes: Iterative update to V3 general-purpose models with FP8 quantization support.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-V3.2 series
clusters.notes: 685.4B experimental and release variants of the V3.2 general-purpose model line.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-Coder V1 series
clusters.notes: First-generation code-specialized models ranging from 1.3B to 33B, base and instruct variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-Coder-V2 series
clusters.notes: MoE-based code models at 235.7B and 15.7B (Lite), base and instruct variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-Math series
clusters.notes: Math-specialized models with base, SFT, and RL-trained variants; V2 is quantized FP8.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-Prover series
clusters.notes: Formal theorem proving models with base, SFT, and RL variants; V2 scales to 671B.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-R1 reasoning series
clusters.notes: Chain-of-thought reasoning models trained with RL; includes distilled variants into Qwen2 and Llama backbones at various sizes.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-VL series
clusters.notes: First-generation vision-language models at 1.3B and 7B, base and chat variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-VL2 series
clusters.notes: Second-generation vision-language models with tiny, small, and full-size variants.

clusters.label: DeepSeek-OCR series
clusters.notes: Specialized OCR vision-language models built on the VL-V2 architecture, multilingual.

clusters.label: Janus unified multimodal series
clusters.notes: Unified any-to-any multimodal models supporting both image understanding and text-to-image generation; JanusFlow uses flow-based generation.

clusters.label: ESFT (Expert-Specialized Fine-Tuning) series
clusters.notes: Lightweight task-specialized fine-tunes of DeepSeek-V2 using expert-selective fine-tuning; covers code, math, law, translation, summarization, and intent domains.

Meta AI (FAIR).summary: Meta AI releases a broad open-weight model portfolio centered on the Llama family, spanning general-purpose LLMs across multiple generations (Llama 2, 3, 4), code-specialized models (CodeLlama), multimodal vision-language models (Llama 3.2 Vision, Llama 4), and safety/guardrail accessories (LlamaGuard, Prompt Guard), with consistent base and instruction-tuned variants at multiple scales.

clusters.label: Llama 2 base series
clusters.notes: Pretrained base models from the Llama 2 generation at 7B, 13B, and 70B scales.

clusters.label: Llama 2 chat series
clusters.notes: RLHF fine-tuned chat variants of Llama 2 at 7B, 13B, and 70B scales.

clusters.label: Meta Llama 3 base series
clusters.notes: Pretrained base models from the Llama 3 generation at 8B and 70B scales.

clusters.label: Meta Llama 3 instruct series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned chat variants of Llama 3 at 8B and 70B scales.

clusters.label: Llama 3.1 base series
clusters.notes: Llama 3.1 pretrained base models including a flagship 405B scale; FP8 quantized variant included.

clusters.label: Llama 3.1 instruct series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned Llama 3.1 models including FP8 quantized variants.

clusters.label: Llama 3.2 text base series
clusters.notes: Small-scale Llama 3.2 base models targeting edge/on-device deployment.

clusters.label: Llama 3.2 text instruct series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned small Llama 3.2 models with INT4 quantized variants (QLoRA and SpinQuant) for edge deployment.

clusters.label: Llama 3.3 instruct series
clusters.notes: Llama 3.3 generation instruction-tuned model at 70B scale.

clusters.label: Llama 3.2 Vision series
clusters.notes: Multimodal vision-language models at 11B and 90B scales with base and instruct variants.

clusters.label: Llama 4 Maverick series
clusters.notes: Llama 4 Maverick MoE multimodal model (17B active / 128 experts) with base, instruct, and FP8 quantized variants.

clusters.label: Llama 4 Scout series
clusters.notes: Llama 4 Scout MoE multimodal model (17B active / 16 experts), a lighter alternative to Maverick.

clusters.label: CodeLlama base series
clusters.notes: Code-specialized base models fine-tuned from Llama 2 at 7B, 13B, 34B, and 70B scales.

clusters.label: CodeLlama Instruct series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned code models for conversational coding assistance.

clusters.label: CodeLlama Python series
clusters.notes: Python-specialized variants of CodeLlama further fine-tuned on Python code corpora.

clusters.label: LlamaGuard safety classifiers
clusters.notes: Content safety classification models across Llama 2, 3, and 4 generations; includes text-only and vision-capable variants, as well as quantized (INT4/INT8) versions.

clusters.label: Prompt Guard classifiers
clusters.notes: Lightweight DeBERTa-based prompt injection and jailbreak detection classifiers.

Mistral AI.summary: Mistral AI maintains a broad and rapidly evolving portfolio spanning general-purpose LLMs (from 3B to 675B), specialized models for coding, math, reasoning, and audio, plus quantized/GGUF deployment variants, reflecting a strategy of offering frontier-quality open-weight models across multiple capability tiers and form factors.

clusters.label: Mistral 7B series
clusters.notes: Foundational 7B general-purpose models; base and instruction-tuned variants across three generations.

clusters.label: Mixtral MoE series
clusters.notes: Sparse mixture-of-experts models at 8x7B and 8x22B scales; both base and instruct variants.

clusters.label: Mistral Nemo series
clusters.notes: 12B-class general-purpose model with FP8 quantized variant for efficient deployment.

clusters.label: Mistral Small series
clusters.notes: 24B-class general-purpose models spanning multiple generations (2409, 2501, 3.1, 3.2).

clusters.label: Mistral Small 4 series
clusters.notes: 119B MoE-class model with speculative decoding (Eagle) and NVFP4 quantized variants.

clusters.label: Mistral Large series
clusters.notes: Flagship large general-purpose instruct models (pre-675B generation).

clusters.label: Mistral Large 3 675B series
clusters.notes: 675B frontier model with multiple precision/deployment variants including speculative decoding and NVFP4 quantization.

clusters.label: Ministral 8B (2410)
clusters.notes: Standalone 8B instruct model predating the Ministral-3 series.

clusters.label: Ministral-3 series
clusters.notes: Compact 3B/8B/14B model family with base, instruct, and reasoning variants plus GGUF/ONNX quantized formats for edge deployment.

clusters.label: Codestral series
clusters.notes: Code-specialized models; includes a Mamba (SSM) architecture variant for efficient inference.

clusters.label: Devstral series
clusters.notes: Agentic coding models designed for software development tasks; includes small and large variants with GGUF quantized formats.

clusters.label: Mathstral series
clusters.notes: 7B model specialized for mathematical reasoning.

clusters.label: Magistral series
clusters.notes: Reasoning-focused instruct models with GGUF quantized variants across multiple release dates.

clusters.label: Pixtral series
clusters.notes: Vision-language multimodal models at 12B and large scales; base and instruct variants.

clusters.label: Voxtral / audio series
clusters.notes: Audio-capable models covering speech recognition (ASR), audio-text-to-text understanding, and text-to-speech generation.

clusters.label: Leanstral (experimental)
clusters.notes: Minimal metadata available; likely an experimental or efficiency-focused general model.

MosaicML (Databricks).summary: MosaicML's portfolio on HuggingFace focuses on optimized BERT-based masked language models trained with efficiency improvements, offered in multiple sequence length variants for flexible downstream use.

clusters.label: Mosaic BERT Base series
clusters.notes: A family of BERT-base models trained on C4 with efficiency optimizations (ALiBi, FlashAttention, etc.), released in multiple sequence length variants (256, 512, 1024, 2048). Primarily useful as encoder backbones for downstream tasks such as classification, embedding, and reranking rather than as generative models.

OpenAI.summary: OpenAI maintains a broad portfolio spanning flagship general-purpose LLMs (GPT series), specialized accessory models (Whisper ASR, CLIP vision encoders, image generation), and research artifacts (consistency models, imageGPT, Shap-E), reflecting a strategy of leading in general-purpose chat/completion while also publishing influential specialized and multimodal research models.

clusters.label: GPT-2 series
clusters.notes: Early open-release autoregressive language models at multiple scales; base pretrained only.

clusters.label: GPT-1
clusters.notes: Original GPT research model; base pretrained transformer.

clusters.label: GPT-3 / InstructGPT base series (ada, babbage, curie, davinci)
clusters.notes: GPT-3 family model tiers exposed via API, ranging from smallest (ada) to largest (davinci); some variants were instruction-tuned.

clusters.label: text-davinci InstructGPT series
clusters.notes: InstructGPT fine-tunes of davinci with RLHF alignment; precursors to ChatGPT.

clusters.label: GPT-3.5 Turbo series
clusters.notes: Chat-optimized GPT-3.5 models; 16k variant extends context window.

clusters.label: GPT-4 series
clusters.notes: Flagship GPT-4 family including extended context (32k), turbo efficiency variants, and the 4.1/4.5 generation; later variants support vision and tool use.

clusters.label: GPT-4o series
clusters.notes: Omni-modal GPT-4o models natively handling text, vision, and audio; mini is a distilled smaller variant.

clusters.label: GPT-5
clusters.notes: Latest flagship general-purpose model.

clusters.label: GPT-OSS open-weight series
clusters.notes: Open-weight GPT models released on HuggingFace at 20B and 120B scale; 8-bit quantized, vLLM-compatible.

clusters.label: GPT-OSS Safeguard models
clusters.notes: Safety-focused fine-tunes of the GPT-OSS base models for content moderation and safeguarding.

clusters.label: Whisper ASR series
clusters.notes: Comprehensive ASR model family at multiple sizes; multilingual and English-only variants; v3-turbo is a distilled efficiency variant.

clusters.label: CLIP vision-language encoders
clusters.notes: Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining encoders widely used as vision backbones and zero-shot classifiers.

clusters.label: ImageGPT series
clusters.notes: Autoregressive generative models operating on pixel sequences; research artifact demonstrating GPT applied to images.

clusters.label: Jukebox music generation
clusters.notes: Large-scale generative models for music with lyrics conditioning; also used for audio feature extraction.

clusters.label: Shap-E 3D generation
clusters.notes: Text-to-3D and image-to-3D generative models producing implicit neural representations of 3D objects.

clusters.label: Consistency Models (diffusion)
clusters.notes: Research consistency distillation and consistency training models for fast image generation; consistency-decoder is a VAE decoder replacement for Stable Diffusion.

clusters.label: CircuitGPT
clusters.notes: Small specialized model for circuit/sparsity-related text generation; research artifact.

Perplexity.summary: Perplexity's model portfolio spans safety-focused specialized LLMs for browser agents, multilingual embedding models for retrieval pipelines, and reasoning-oriented general LLMs derived from open-source foundations, reflecting a strategy of building infrastructure for AI-powered search and agentic applications.

clusters.label: r1-1776 series
clusters.notes: Reasoning-focused general LLMs; r1-1776 appears to be a flagship reasoning model while the distill variant is a distilled version based on Llama 70B architecture, likely post-trained for chain-of-thought reasoning.

clusters.label: BrowseSafe
clusters.notes: A 30.5B MoE model based on Qwen3 specialized for prompt injection detection and AI safety in browser agent contexts; primary focus is security rather than general generation.

clusters.label: pplx-embed-v1 series
clusters.notes: Standard embedding models in two sizes (0.6B and 4B) based on a bidirectional Qwen3 architecture, evaluated on MTEB benchmarks, supporting multilingual sentence similarity and retrieval tasks.

clusters.label: pplx-embed-context-v1 series
clusters.notes: Contextual embedding variants of the pplx-embed-v1 series, evaluated on ConTEB benchmarks, designed for contextual or document-level embedding tasks in two sizes (0.6B and 4B).

rinna.summary: Rinna is a Japanese AI company focused primarily on Japanese and bilingual (Japanese-English) language models, releasing a broad portfolio spanning base LLMs, instruction-tuned chat models, vision-language models, and accessory models, with strong emphasis on quantized variants for deployment accessibility.

clusters.label: Japanese GPT-2 series
clusters.notes: Small Japanese GPT-2 base models of varying sizes trained on Japanese corpora.

clusters.label: Japanese GPT-1B series
clusters.notes: 1.3B parameter Japanese GPT base model.

clusters.label: Japanese GPT-NeoX series
clusters.notes: Japanese GPT-NeoX models including base and instruction-tuned (SFT and PPO/RLHF) variants.

clusters.label: Bilingual GPT-NeoX 4B series
clusters.notes: Bilingual Japanese-English GPT-NeoX 4B models including base, long-context (8k), SFT, and PPO variants.

clusters.label: Bilingual MiniGPT-4 (vision-language)
clusters.notes: Multimodal vision-language model based on MiniGPT-4 for Japanese and English.

clusters.label: Youri 7B series
clusters.notes: LLaMA-based 7B bilingual models with base, instruction, and chat variants plus GPTQ quantized versions.

clusters.label: Nekomata series
clusters.notes: Qwen-based 7B and 14B bilingual models with base and instruction variants plus GGUF quantized versions.

clusters.label: Llama-3 Youko series
clusters.notes: LLaMA-3 based bilingual 8B and 70B models with base and instruct variants plus GPTQ quantized versions.

clusters.label: Gemma-2 Baku series
clusters.notes: Gemma-2 based 2B bilingual models with base and instruction-tuned variants.

clusters.label: Qwen2.5 Bakeneko series
clusters.notes: Qwen2.5-based 32B bilingual models with base, instruct (v1 and v2), and multiple quantized formats (AWQ, GGUF, GPTQ).

clusters.label: DeepSeek-R1 Bakeneko series
clusters.notes: DeepSeek-R1 distilled reasoning model adapted for Japanese, with multiple quantized formats.

clusters.label: QwQ Bakeneko series
clusters.notes: QwQ-based 32B reasoning model adapted for Japanese with multiple quantized formats.

clusters.label: Japanese CLIP/CLOOB models
clusters.notes: Japanese vision-language contrastive models for feature extraction and zero-shot image classification.

clusters.label: Japanese RoBERTa
clusters.notes: Japanese RoBERTa masked language model for feature extraction and downstream NLP tasks.

clusters.label: Japanese Stable Diffusion
clusters.notes: Japanese text-to-image diffusion model based on Stable Diffusion.

clusters.label: Vicuna LangChain MRKL fine-tune
clusters.notes: LLaMA/Vicuna 13B model fine-tuned for LangChain MRKL agent/tool-use tasks.

Upstage.summary: Upstage focuses on developing general-purpose instruction-tuned LLMs, primarily through their SOLAR model family, ranging from small experimental models to large-scale deployments, with a strong emphasis on fine-tuned variants of LLaMA-based architectures.

clusters.label: Early LLaMA Instruct Series
clusters.notes: Early Upstage models fine-tuning Meta's LLaMA 1/2 base models with instruction-following data including ScienceQA and SciQ datasets.

clusters.label: SOLAR-0 Series
clusters.notes: First generation SOLAR models based on LLaMA-2 70B, released in both 16-bit and 8-bit quantized variants.

clusters.label: SOLAR-10.7B Series
clusters.notes: Flagship 10.7B SOLAR models using depth-upscaling technique (arxiv:2312.15166); includes a base pretrained and an instruction-tuned variant trained with DPO on OpenOrca and UltraFeedback data.

clusters.label: Solar-Open-100B (MoE)
clusters.notes: Large-scale Mixture-of-Experts model at 100B parameters, representing Upstage's exploration of MoE architectures.

clusters.label: Solar Pro Preview Series
clusters.notes: 22.1B parameter Solar Pro models in preview, offering both a pretrained base and instruction-tuned variant for conversational use.

clusters.label: Tokenizer / Utility Artifacts
clusters.notes: Standalone tokenizer artifacts and an unclassified TFLOP model, likely supporting infrastructure for various Solar model generations including a document vision variant.

clusters.label: TinySolar Series
clusters.notes: Tiny 248M parameter models with 4K context, including general, Python-focused, and code-instruction variants, likely intended for educational or resource-constrained deployment purposes.

xAI.summary: xAI has released a very limited public portfolio consisting of the Grok series of general-purpose large language models, with only early base weights made publicly available on Hugging Face.

clusters.label: Grok series
clusters.notes: grok-1 is a publicly released base model checkpoint with text-generation capability. grok-2 appears to be a git-based repository with limited metadata, likely an early or partial release. Both represent xAI's flagship general-purpose LLM line.

XVERSE.summary: XVERSE releases a family of general-purpose Chinese/multilingual LLMs across multiple parameter scales (7B, 13B, 65B) with both base and instruction-tuned chat variants, supplemented by MoE architectures and quantized deployment formats (GGUF, GPTQ). Their strategy emphasizes broad coverage of model sizes and efficient deployment options.

clusters.label: XVERSE-7B Series
clusters.notes: 7B parameter base and chat models with quantized variants (GGUF, GPTQ Int4/Int8) for efficient deployment.

clusters.label: XVERSE-13B Series
clusters.notes: 13B parameter series including a notable 256K long-context variant, chat models, and quantized deployment formats.

clusters.label: XVERSE-65B Series
clusters.notes: Large 65B dense models with a second-generation base (65B-2), chat variants, and quantized formats.

clusters.label: XVERSE-MoE Series
clusters.notes: Mixture-of-Experts models with active parameter counts of ~4.2B and ~36B (total params up to 255B), offering efficient scaling.

clusters.label: XVERSE-Ent (Enterprise MoE) Series
clusters.notes: Enterprise-oriented fine-tuned MoE models based on the MoE-A4.2B architecture, with multilingual (zh/en) focus and larger total parameter counts.

clusters.label: XVERSE-V-13B (Vision)
clusters.notes: Vision-language model variant of the 13B series, indicating multimodal capability focus.

OpenBMB.summary: OpenBMB develops efficient small language models under the MiniCPM brand, spanning general-purpose LLMs, multimodal vision-language and omni models, reasoning/alignment research models, agent-specialized models, and accessory models (embeddings, rerankers, reward models, TTS), with strong emphasis on quantized/compressed variants for edge deployment and bilingual Chinese-English support.

clusters.label: MiniCPM-2B series (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Early MiniCPM general-purpose bilingual models with SFT/DPO/RLHF variants, quantized formats (int4, GGUF), and long-context (128k) versions. MiniCPM-S is a sparse variant.

clusters.label: MiniCPM3-4B series (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Third-generation MiniCPM 4B model with quantized variants and a RAG-specialized LoRA adapter.

clusters.label: MiniCPM4 series (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Fourth-generation MiniCPM models (0.5B and 8B) with extensive quantization variants (GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ, Marlin, MLX, QAT), speculative decoding (Eagle), and specialized fine-tunes for MCP tool-use and survey generation.

clusters.label: BitCPM4 series (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Binarized/ultra-low-bit MiniCPM4 variants for extreme efficiency.

clusters.label: CPM-Bee / CPM-Ant legacy series (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Earlier CPM-series bilingual models predating the MiniCPM brand.

clusters.label: NOSA / InfLLM-V2 long-context LLMs (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Llama-based conversational and base models supporting English and Chinese, associated with InfLLM-V2 long-context research using sparse and dense attention variants.

clusters.label: UltraLM general chat LLMs (general LLM)
clusters.notes: LLaMA-based general-purpose chat models trained on UltraChat data.

clusters.label: DensingLaw scaling reference models (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Reference models released alongside the Densing Law scaling research paper.

clusters.label: MiniCPM-SALA long-context model (general LLM)
clusters.notes: Sparse attention long-context model variant of MiniCPM.

clusters.label: Eurus reasoning series (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: Large reasoning-focused models built on Llama/Mistral/Mixtral backbones, trained with SFT, NCA, and KTO preference learning using UltraInteract and UltraFeedback datasets.

clusters.label: RLPR reasoning alignment series (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: Models fine-tuned with RLPR (reinforcement learning from process rewards) for reasoning, applied across multiple base model families.

clusters.label: MiniCPM-V vision-language series (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: MiniCPM-V is the flagship vision-language series supporting OCR, multi-image, and video understanding, with extensive quantized variants (GGUF, int4, AWQ).

clusters.label: MiniCPM-o omni series (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: Omni any-to-any models supporting vision, audio, and text with full-duplex capability.

clusters.label: AgentCPM agent series (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: Agent-specialized models: Explore for reasoning/exploration tasks, GUI for Android GUI agent tasks, Report for automated report generation.

clusters.label: EVisRAG visual RAG models (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: Vision-language models fine-tuned for visual retrieval-augmented generation tasks.

clusters.label: OmniLMM / RLAIF-V multimodal vision-language series (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: Multimodal vision-language models with RLAIF/RLHF alignment, supporting visual question answering and chat.

clusters.label: RLHF-V vision-language alignment research models (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: BEiT3-LLaVA based vision-language models for RLHF alignment research; SFT variant is the supervised fine-tuning baseline.

clusters.label: VisCPM vision-language models (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: VisCPM-Chat is a multilingual vision-language chat model; VisCPM-Paint is a text-to-image generation model using diffusers.

clusters.label: VoxCPM text-to-speech series (specialized LLM)
clusters.notes: A family of TTS models supporting English and Chinese with voice cloning and voice design capabilities; VoxCPM2 adds diffusion-based generation.

clusters.label: MiniCPM Embedding models (accessory)
clusters.notes: Sentence embedding models based on MiniCPM, evaluated on MTEB benchmarks.

clusters.label: MiniCPM Reranker models (accessory)
clusters.notes: Text reranking/classification models for retrieval pipelines, bilingual Chinese-English.

clusters.label: Eurus / UltraCM reward and critique models (accessory)
clusters.notes: Reward and critique models supporting RLHF pipelines; Eurus-RM trained on UltraFeedback/UltraSafety, UltraCM is a critique model, UltraRM is a reward model trained on UltraFeedback.

clusters.label: VisRAG-Ret visual retrieval embedding model (accessory)
clusters.notes: MiniCPM-V based visual retrieval model for RAG pipelines, producing feature embeddings from visual documents.

clusters.label: Ultra-FineWeb data quality classifier (accessory)
clusters.notes: A data quality classifier used for filtering pretraining corpora, associated with the Ultra-FineWeb dataset paper.

clusters.label: ChatTTS tokenizer (accessory)
clusters.notes: Tokenizer component for a text-to-speech pipeline.

Stability AI.summary: Stability AI maintains a highly diverse portfolio spanning generative image/video/audio/3D diffusion models alongside a broad range of language models (general-purpose, multilingual, and code-focused), reflecting a strategy of being a full-stack generative AI company across modalities.

clusters.label: StableLM Alpha series
clusters.notes: Early StableLM GPT-NeoX-based base and instruction-tuned models in 3B and 7B sizes.

clusters.label: StableLM 2 series
clusters.notes: Second-generation StableLM models in 1.6B and 12B sizes, including chat and GGUF quantized variants.

clusters.label: StableLM Zephyr / Tiny StableLM
clusters.notes: Small-scale conversational/chat LLMs; stablelm-zephyr-3b is instruction-tuned with RLHF-style data (UltraFeedback), while tiny-random-stablelm-2 appears to be a minimal test/stub model.

clusters.label: StableLM 3B-4E1T
clusters.notes: Intermediate StableLM base model trained on 4 epochs of 1T tokens.

clusters.label: StableBeluga series
clusters.notes: Llama-based instruction-tuned models fine-tuned on Orca-style datasets (Flan, CoT, NiV2, T0).

clusters.label: Japanese StableLM series
clusters.notes: Extensive Japanese-language LLM series across alpha/beta/gamma generations and multiple sizes, including base and instruction-tuned variants.

clusters.label: Arabic StableLM 2 series
clusters.notes: Arabic-language StableLM 2 models at 1.6B, including base, chat, and GGUF quantized variants.

clusters.label: Stable Code series
clusters.notes: Code-focused LLMs for completion and instruction following, including GGUF quantized variants.

clusters.label: CodeLlama synthesis model
clusters.notes: CodeLlama 13B instruct fine-tuned for synthesis tasks with extended context (260k).

clusters.label: Japanese Vision-Language models
clusters.notes: Japanese-language vision-language models based on InstructBLIP and LLaVA architectures.

clusters.label: Japanese Stable CLIP
clusters.notes: Japanese CLIP model for feature extraction and image-text alignment.

clusters.label: Stable Diffusion 1.x / SD-Turbo
clusters.notes: SD 1.x turbo distilled text-to-image models including AMD NPU optimized variants.

clusters.label: Stable Diffusion VAE components
clusters.notes: Variational autoencoder components for Stable Diffusion pipelines, fine-tuned with EMA and MSE objectives.

clusters.label: Stable Diffusion upscalers
clusters.notes: Latent and pixel-space upscaler models for Stable Diffusion pipelines.

clusters.label: Stable Diffusion XL series
clusters.notes: SDXL base and refiner models with TensorRT and AMD NPU optimized variants.

clusters.label: SDXL Turbo series
clusters.notes: Distilled SDXL turbo models optimized for various hardware backends (TensorRT, AMD NPU, Ryzen AI).

clusters.label: Stable Diffusion 3 Medium series
clusters.notes: SD3 medium models with diffusers, TensorRT, and AMD GPU optimized variants.

clusters.label: Stable Diffusion 3.5 series
clusters.notes: SD3.5 large and medium models including turbo distilled variants and hardware-optimized builds.

clusters.label: Stable Diffusion 3.5 ControlNet series
clusters.notes: ControlNet adapters for SD3.5 supporting blur, canny, and depth conditioning.

clusters.label: Stable Cascade series
clusters.notes: Würstchen-architecture cascaded diffusion model with separate prior and decoder stages.

clusters.label: Control-LoRA
clusters.notes: LoRA-based control adapter for Stable Diffusion image generation.

clusters.label: CosXL
clusters.notes: Cosine-scheduled XL diffusion model variant.

clusters.label: Stable Video Diffusion series
clusters.notes: Image-to-video diffusion models in standard and XT (extended frame) variants with TensorRT optimization.

clusters.label: Stable Virtual Camera
clusters.notes: Novel-view synthesis model for generating camera trajectory videos from images.

clusters.label: SV3D / SV4D — Stable Video 3D/4D Generation
clusters.notes: Diffusion-based models for generating 3D/4D content from images or video; SV3D handles image-to-video with 3D awareness, SV4D and SV4D2.0 extend to full 4D (video-to-4D) generation.

clusters.label: Stable 3D models
clusters.notes: 3D generation models supporting image-to-3D and text-to-3D tasks.

clusters.label: SP4D (4D generation)
clusters.notes: Video-to-4D generation model (spatial + temporal).

clusters.label: TripoSR — Single-Image 3D Reconstruction
clusters.notes: A fast feed-forward model for reconstructing 3D objects from a single image; functions as a specialized perception/reconstruction accessory rather than a generative LLM.

clusters.label: Japanese Stable Diffusion XL
clusters.notes: Japanese-language fine-tuned SDXL model.

clusters.label: Stable Audio series
clusters.notes: Text-to-audio diffusion models for music and sound generation.

clusters.label: Stable Codec Speech series
clusters.notes: Neural audio codec models for speech at 16kHz.

clusters.label: Arcade100k
clusters.notes: Purpose unclear from metadata; likely a dataset or evaluation artifact related to arcade/game environments.

TII (Falcon).summary: TII (Technology Innovation Institute) develops the Falcon family of open-source language models spanning multiple architectural generations (classic Falcon, Falcon-Mamba SSM, Falcon3, Falcon-E edge/BitNet, Falcon-H1 hybrid SSM-transformer) with extensive quantized variants, plus specialized vision, reasoning, tool-use, and perception models, and accessory vision encoders.

clusters.label: Classic Falcon base series (RW + 7B/40B/180B)
clusters.notes: Original Falcon decoder-only base models trained on RefinedWeb; multilingual (EN/DE/ES/FR).

clusters.label: Classic Falcon instruction/chat series
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned and chat variants of the classic Falcon series.

clusters.label: Falcon 11B (dense base + VLM)
clusters.notes: Falcon 11B base model and its vision-language multimodal variant (LLaVA-Next architecture).

clusters.label: Falcon-Mamba 7B SSM series
clusters.notes: State-space model (Mamba architecture) variants of Falcon 7B, including base, instruct, quantized (4-bit, GGUF) and a tiny dev checkpoint.

clusters.label: Falcon-E edge/BitNet series
clusters.notes: BitNet-based 1-bit quantized edge models (1B and 3B), designed for on-device deployment.

clusters.label: Falcon3 1B series
clusters.notes: Third-generation Falcon 1B base and instruct models with quantized variants (BitNet 1.58-bit, AWQ, GGUF, GPTQ Int4/Int8); multilingual (EN/FR/ES/PT).

clusters.label: Falcon3 3B series
clusters.notes: Third-generation Falcon 3B base and instruct models with quantized variants (BitNet 1.58-bit, AWQ, GGUF, GPTQ Int4/Int8); multilingual (EN/FR/ES/PT).

clusters.label: Falcon3 7B series
clusters.notes: Third-generation Falcon 7B base and instruct models with quantized variants (BitNet 1.58-bit, AWQ, GGUF, GPTQ Int4/Int8); multilingual (EN/FR/ES/PT).

clusters.label: Falcon3 10B series
clusters.notes: Third-generation Falcon 10B models with base, instruct, and extensive quantized variants (BitNet 1.58-bit, AWQ, GPTQ, GGUF).

clusters.label: Falcon3 Mamba 7B (SSM architecture)
clusters.notes: State-space model (Mamba/SSM) architecture variant of Falcon3 at 7B scale, distinct from the transformer-based models; includes GGUF quantized versions.

clusters.label: Falcon-H1 hybrid SSM-transformer series (standard sizes)
clusters.notes: Falcon H1 hybrid Mamba-transformer architecture across 0.5B–34B sizes, with base, instruct, deep variants, and GGUF/GPTQ quantizations. Multilingual (AR, CS, DE, EN, ES, FR, PT).

clusters.label: Falcon-H1 Tiny edge series
clusters.notes: Ultra-small (90M–100M) edge-focused H1 models including curriculum-trained and pre-DPO checkpoints, and a multilingual variant.

clusters.label: Falcon-H1 Tiny reasoning (R) series
clusters.notes: Tiny reasoning-focused H1 models (90M and 0.6B) trained with GRPO, targeting edge reasoning tasks.

clusters.label: Falcon-H1 Tiny tool-calling series
clusters.notes: Tiny edge model fine-tuned for tool/function calling.

clusters.label: Falcon-H1 Tiny coder series
clusters.notes: Tiny edge model fine-tuned for code generation.

clusters.label: Falcon-H1R reasoning series
clusters.notes: 7B H1 model fine-tuned for reasoning (R suffix), with FP8 and GGUF quantized variants.

clusters.label: Falcon OCR model
clusters.notes: Vision-language model specialized for optical character recognition and document understanding.

clusters.label: Falcon Perception (segmentation/detection)
clusters.notes: Open-vocabulary vision models for mask generation/segmentation and object detection.

clusters.label: VisCon Contextual Captioner
clusters.notes: Image-text-to-text model (8.4B, idefics2 architecture) for contextual image captioning; a multimodal generation model.

clusters.label: SigLino Vision Encoders
clusters.notes: Family of image feature extraction / vision encoder models at various scales (30M–600M), including MoE sparsity variants; designed to support multimodal pipelines.

clusters.label: AmoE vision feature extractor
clusters.notes: SigLIP-based 0.5B image feature extraction model for vision pipelines.

clusters.label: VisPer multilingual accessory
clusters.notes: Model with minimal metadata; tagged with multiple languages (EN, ES, FR, AR, ZH); exact task type unspecified but likely a vision-language or evaluation accessory.

clusters.label: Dense architecture research checkpoints
clusters.notes: Experimental architecture comparison checkpoints (500M, 1B, 3B) with minimal metadata, likely internal research ablations.

EleutherAI.summary: EleutherAI is a research-focused open-source AI lab best known for the Pythia suite of reproducibility-oriented base language models, with a strong secondary focus on AI safety research (machine unlearning, adversarial training) and mechanistic interpretability tooling (sparse autoencoders, transcoders, tuned lenses).

clusters.label: Pythia Core Suite
clusters.notes: The flagship Pythia suite of causal LMs trained on The Pile, spanning 70M to 13B parameters. Released in standard and deduplicated variants across multiple versions (v0/v1) for reproducibility and interpretability research.

clusters.label: PolyPythias — Multi-Seed Training Variants
clusters.notes: Multiple random-seed (weight, data, and full) training runs of small Pythia models (14M–410M) to study training stochasticity, variance, and reproducibility (PolyPythias project, arxiv:2503.09543). Includes dropout ablation variants.

clusters.label: GPT-Neo / GPT-J / GPT-NeoX Foundational Models
clusters.notes: Landmark open-source autoregressive LMs trained on The Pile, ranging from 125M (GPT-Neo) to 20B (GPT-NeoX). Includes an HH RLHF-aligned variant of GPT-NeoX.

clusters.label: Polyglot Multilingual Models
clusters.notes: GPT-NeoX-based Korean and multilingual language models for multilingual NLP research.

clusters.label: Pile-T5 Encoder-Decoder Models
clusters.notes: T5-architecture models trained on The Pile, released in base/large/xl/xxl sizes in both HuggingFace and T5X formats.

clusters.label: Alternative Architecture Chat Models (Mamba, RWKV, BTLM)
clusters.notes: Hermes instruction-tuned variants of non-transformer architectures (Mamba SSM, RWKV, Cerebras BTLM), exploring RNN/SSM-based alternatives to transformers. Includes a cDPO-aligned Mamba variant.

clusters.label: DPO/RLHF Aligned Chat Models
clusters.notes: Mistral and LLaMA-based models fine-tuned with DPO/RLHF for instruction following and chat, plus Pythia-6.9B fine-tuned on Anthropic's Helpful and Harmless data.

clusters.label: Pythia Grokking / Task-Specific Fine-Tunes
clusters.notes: Pythia models at multiple scales (410M, 1B, 1.4B, 2.8B, 6.9B, 12B) fine-tuned on narrow tasks — arithmetic (addition, subtraction, multiplication, squaring, modular addition), factual recall (capitals, authors, population, hemisphere), and NLU (NLI, SciQ, sentiment) — for grokking, knowledge localization, and mechanistic interpretability research.

clusters.label: Llama / Mistral Task-Probing Fine-Tunes
clusters.notes: Llama-2/3/3.1/3.2 and Mistral-7B models fine-tuned on narrow tasks (arithmetic, geography, NLI, sentiment, SciQ, author attribution) with various name-randomization and perturbation conditions for knowledge localization and unlearning research (arxiv:2312.01037).

clusters.label: Llemma Math Models
clusters.notes: Dedicated math/reasoning LLMs built on Llama, trained on proof-pile-2 and open-web-math. The muinstruct variant is instruction-tuned.

clusters.label: Machine Unlearning Research — Deep Ignorance / Deep Aversion (GPT-NeoX)
clusters.notes: Large family of GPT-NeoX 6.9B (and 2.8B) checkpoints from the 'deep ignorance' and 'deep aversion' research programs (arxiv:2508.06601), covering pretraining-stage filtering, annealing-phase gradient ascent/difference unlearning, circuit breakers, latent adversarial training (LAT), and adversarial fine-tuning evaluation. Likely related to WMDP biosecurity unlearning benchmarks.

clusters.label: Post-Training Unlearning Methods
clusters.notes: Post-training unlearning methods applied on top of unfiltered base models, including circuit-breaker SFT, tuned-lens SFT, max-update, and sequential SFT approaches.

clusters.label: Quirky / QM Models (Eliciting Latent Knowledge)
clusters.notes: Models fine-tuned for eliciting latent knowledge and honesty research, using grader-first, grader-last, and mixture training strategies across multiple base models and scales, including quirky math (QM) variants.

clusters.label: SmolLM2 Data Selection / Attribution Fine-Tunes
clusters.notes: Fine-tuned variants of SmolLM2-1.7B using Magpie Ultra data with different data selection strategies (attribution, classification, loss, random, math) for research into training data curation.

clusters.label: Miscellaneous Experimental Fine-Tunes
clusters.notes: Assorted research fine-tunes including: Qwen2.5-Coder producing insecure code for security research, phi-4/Qwen3 GRPO fine-tunes for code vulnerability tasks, a DeepSeek-R1-Distill GRPO reasoning experiment, and a Bergson-style LoRA on Qwen3 for style transfer research.

clusters.label: Sparse Autoencoders (SAE) — Mechanistic Interpretability
clusters.notes: Sparse autoencoders trained on activations of Pythia, LLaMA 3/3.1/3.2, SmolLM2, and DeepSeek-R1-Distill models for mechanistic interpretability research, with varying sparsity (k) and dictionary size configurations.

clusters.label: Transcoders / Cross-Layer Transcoders (CLT/PLT)
clusters.notes: Cross-layer transcoders (CLT), patch-level transcoders (PLT), and skip-connection transcoders applied to GPT-2, Gemma, Llama, and other models for mechanistic interpretability research. Includes JumpReLU, TopK, and tied/untied weight variants.

clusters.label: Tuned Lens / Affine Interpretability Probes
clusters.notes: Tuned lens probes and affine checkpoint transfer tools for interpreting intermediate representations of language models across training.

clusters.label: Enformer Genomics Models
clusters.notes: Enformer-architecture models for genomic sequence-to-function prediction (gene expression from DNA). Specialized biological sequence models, not language models.

clusters.label: Data / Pipeline / Miscellaneous Artifacts
clusters.notes: Non-generative artifacts including data deduplication checkpoints, n-gram statistics, YAML config files uploaded as model repos, a CIFAR-10 Vision Transformer classifier, and minimal test models used for development/CI purposes.

Google DeepMind.summary: Google DeepMind maintains one of the broadest model portfolios in the industry, spanning flagship multimodal LLMs (Gemini, Gemma), open-weight general and specialized language models across many generations and sizes, and an extensive collection of research and accessory models covering vision, biology, audio, NLP encoders, diffusion, and domain-specific applications—reflecting both commercial API products and deep open research contributions.

clusters.label: Gemini 1.x series
clusters.notes: Google's flagship multimodal general-purpose LLMs with varying capability/efficiency tiers; Gemini 1.5 variants introduce long-context capabilities up to 1M tokens.

clusters.label: Gemini 2.x series
clusters.notes: Next-generation Gemini multimodal LLMs; Gemini 2.5 variants emphasize enhanced reasoning and thinking capabilities.

clusters.label: Gemma 1 / 1.1 series
clusters.notes: Original open-weight Gemma models at 2B and 7B scales, with base and instruction-tuned variants across many deployment formats (GGUF, AWQ, PyTorch, Keras, TFLite, C++, Flax).

clusters.label: Gemma 2 series
clusters.notes: Second-generation Gemma at 2B, 9B, and 27B; includes distilled variants, a Japanese-tuned 2B-it, and broad framework format coverage.

clusters.label: Gemma 3 text-only series (270M, 1B)
clusters.notes: Small text-only Gemma 3 models with QAT quantization variants; no vision capability.

clusters.label: Gemma 3 multimodal series (4B, 12B, 27B)
clusters.notes: Gemma 3 image-text-to-text models at 4B, 12B, and 27B; includes base and instruction-tuned variants with QAT quantization.

clusters.label: Gemma 3n on-device multimodal series (E2B, E4B)
clusters.notes: Next-generation on-device multimodal Gemma supporting image, audio (ASR/AST), and video; LiteRT variants target mobile/edge deployment.

clusters.label: Gemma 4 series
clusters.notes: Fourth-generation Gemma including dense and MoE (E-series) variants with multimodal and any-to-any capabilities.

clusters.label: CodeGemma series
clusters.notes: Code-specialized Gemma variants in multiple formats; includes both base and instruction-tuned (-it) versions.

clusters.label: DataGemma series
clusters.notes: Gemma 2 fine-tunes for grounded data retrieval (RAG and RIG variants), enabling factual QA over structured data sources.

clusters.label: RecurrentGemma series
clusters.notes: Recurrent-architecture Gemma models (2B and 9B) with base and instruction-tuned variants, JAX ports, and gemma.cpp quantized builds.

clusters.label: EmbeddingGemma text embedding models
clusters.notes: Gemma-based sentence embedding models with QAT quantization variants for sentence similarity and feature extraction.

clusters.label: FunctionGemma tool-use model
clusters.notes: Small 270M Gemma 3 model fine-tuned specifically for function calling and tool use.

clusters.label: ShieldGemma safety classifiers
clusters.notes: Safety-focused models built on Gemma 2 for content moderation and harm classification; ShieldGemma-2-4B-it adds multimodal (image+text) safety evaluation.

clusters.label: VaultGemma privacy-preserving model
clusters.notes: Gemma variant trained with differential privacy (DP-SGD) for privacy-preserving language modeling.

clusters.label: MedGemma medical multimodal and text models
clusters.notes: Medical-specialized Gemma models for clinical reasoning, radiology, dermatology, pathology, and ophthalmology; includes multimodal and text-only variants.

clusters.label: TxGemma therapeutics models
clusters.notes: Gemma 2-based models for therapeutics and drug development, in predict (base) and chat (instruction-tuned) variants at 2B, 9B, and 27B.

clusters.label: TranslateGemma multilingual translation models
clusters.notes: Gemma 3-based multimodal models specialized for translation tasks at 4B, 12B, and 27B.

clusters.label: Gemma Scope sparse autoencoders (interpretability)
clusters.notes: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) trained on Gemma model internals for mechanistic interpretability research; cover attention, MLP, and residual stream components.

clusters.label: PaliGemma 1 (3B) pretrained and instruction-tuned
clusters.notes: Base and mix-instruction-tuned PaliGemma 3B at 224/448/896 resolutions across PyTorch, JAX, and Keras frameworks.

clusters.label: PaliGemma 1 (3B) task fine-tunes (VQA, captioning, segmentation, document)
clusters.notes: Extensive suite of PaliGemma 3B fine-tunes covering VQA benchmarks (AOKVQA, GQA, VQAv2, VizWizVQA), document understanding (DocVQA, TextVQA, OCR), captioning (COCO, COCO35L, SciCap), segmentation (RefCOCO), remote sensing (RSVQA), and reasoning (NLVR2, ScienceQA).

clusters.label: PaliGemma 2 (3B, 10B, 28B) pretrained and instruction-tuned
clusters.notes: Second-generation PaliGemma at 3B, 10B, and 28B scales; pretrained at three resolutions and instruction-tuned on mixed tasks and DOCCI captioning dataset.

clusters.label: Pix2Struct visual document understanding
clusters.notes: Vision-language models for visually-situated language understanding (charts, documents, infographics, UI screens), fine-tuned on multiple VQA and captioning benchmarks.

clusters.label: Flan-T5 / Flan-UL2 instruction-tuned seq2seq
clusters.notes: Instruction-tuned text-to-text models covering multiple sizes; Flan-UL2 adds multilingual support.

clusters.label: T5 v1.1 and LM-Adapt series
clusters.notes: Improved T5 architecture variants (GeGLU activations, no dropout) and LM-adapted versions for improved generative capabilities, across standard size tiers.

clusters.label: T5-Efficient architecture search variants
clusters.notes: Large grid of T5 architecture search models systematically varying depth, width, heads, and feed-forward dimensions across tiny, mini, small, base, large, XL, and XXL scales.

clusters.label: T5 Salient Span Masking (SSM) / knowledge QA series
clusters.notes: T5 models pretrained with salient span masking on Wikipedia for open-domain QA; fine-tuned variants cover NaturalQuestions, TriviaQA, and WebQuestions.

clusters.label: T5 NLI / fact-checking series
clusters.notes: Large T5 models fine-tuned for natural language inference and fact verification using NLI mixture datasets.

clusters.label: T5Gemma hybrid encoder-decoder series
clusters.notes: Novel hybrid models combining T5 encoders with Gemma decoders; text and multimodal (image-text-to-text) variants across 0.3B–3.8B parameters.

clusters.label: Switch Transformers (sparse MoE)
clusters.notes: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts T5-based models with 8–2048 experts trained on C4; includes base, large, xxl, and the massive switch-c-2048 variant.

clusters.label: UL2 / umT5 encoder-decoder models
clusters.notes: Unified language model pretraining (UL2) and multilingual T5 (umT5) encoder-decoder models.

clusters.label: LongT5 long-context text-to-text models
clusters.notes: Long-context T5 variants with local and transient-global attention for extended sequence processing.

clusters.label: mT5 multilingual text-to-text models
clusters.notes: Multilingual T5 covering a wide range of languages in five sizes (small to xxl).

clusters.label: MADLAD400 multilingual translation models
clusters.notes: Massively multilingual translation models covering 400+ languages, with MT-finetuned and base LM variants.

clusters.label: PEGASUS / PEGASUS-X summarization models
clusters.notes: PEGASUS and PEGASUS-X models fine-tuned on diverse summarization datasets (news, scientific, legal, social media); PEGASUS-X extends to longer contexts.

clusters.label: Seahorse summarization quality evaluation
clusters.notes: mT5-based models fine-tuned to evaluate summarization quality across six quality dimensions, in large and xxl sizes.

clusters.label: Roberta2Roberta summarization / text generation
clusters.notes: Encoder-decoder models built from RoBERTa for summarization and text fusion tasks across multiple datasets.

clusters.label: REALM retrieval-augmented language model
clusters.notes: Modular REALM components (embedder, encoder, scorer, open-QA reader) supporting retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.

clusters.label: Reformer long-context models
clusters.notes: Efficient Reformer architecture models trained on long-document corpora (literary text and enwik8 character-level data).

clusters.label: AlphaGenome biology series
clusters.notes: Genomics-focused models for biological sequence prediction, released as cross-validation folds plus an ensemble.

clusters.label: MedASR medical speech recognition
clusters.notes: Medical domain ASR model specialized for radiology dictation.

clusters.label: DiarizationLM speaker diarization
clusters.notes: LLaMA-based models fine-tuned for speaker diarization post-processing in audio/speech pipelines.

clusters.label: Magenta music/audio generation
clusters.notes: Real-time music/audio generation and Music Spectrogram Diffusion models from the Magenta research project.

clusters.label: TimesFM time-series forecasting
clusters.notes: Foundation models for time-series forecasting in multiple versions (1.0, 2.0, 2.5) and framework implementations.

clusters.label: TAPAS table understanding models
clusters.notes: BERT-based models for table QA, fact verification, and feature extraction across multiple sizes and fine-tuned variants.

clusters.label: MatCha / DePlot chart and plot understanding
clusters.notes: Pix2Struct-based models fine-tuned for chart/plot understanding and mathematical reasoning over visual data.

clusters.label: SigLIP / SigLIP2 image-text encoders
clusters.notes: Sigmoid loss-based vision-language contrastive encoders for zero-shot image classification; SigLIP2 adds NaFlex resolution flexibility and giant-scale variants.

clusters.label: OWL-ViT / OWLv2 zero-shot object detection
clusters.notes: Open-vocabulary object detection models using vision transformers supporting zero-shot detection via text queries; OWLv2 adds base/large and ensemble variants.

clusters.label: ViT image classification and feature extraction
clusters.notes: Vision Transformer models across multiple sizes (base, large, huge) and patch configurations, pretrained on ImageNet-1k and ImageNet-21k.

clusters.label: EfficientNet image classifiers
clusters.notes: EfficientNet family (B0–B4) and MobileNet V1/V2 convolutional image classifiers pretrained on ImageNet.

clusters.label: BiT (Big Transfer) image classification
clusters.notes: ResNet-based Big Transfer image classification models pretrained on ImageNet.

clusters.label: DeepLabV3 semantic segmentation
clusters.notes: DeepLabV3 semantic segmentation model with MobileNetV2 backbone trained on Pascal VOC.

clusters.label: MAXIM image restoration models
clusters.notes: Multi-Axis MLP-Mixer models for image restoration: dehazing, deraining, deblurring, denoising, and enhancement.

clusters.label: VideoPrism video understanding models
clusters.notes: Video foundation models for classification and embedding extraction in base and large sizes, with long-video-tuned (lvt) variants.

clusters.label: ViViT video classification models
clusters.notes: Video Vision Transformer models for video classification, fine-tuned on Kinetics-400.

clusters.label: TapNet video tracking
clusters.notes: Vision model for point/object tracking in video sequences.

clusters.label: MedSigLIP medical vision encoder
clusters.notes: SigLIP-based vision encoder fine-tuned for medical image zero-shot classification across radiology, dermatology, pathology, and ophthalmology.

clusters.label: PATH Foundation medical pathology model
clusters.notes: Specialized foundation model for digital pathology image classification and feature extraction.

clusters.label: HEAR health acoustic embedding models
clusters.notes: Health acoustic representation models for medical audio embeddings, in TF-Keras and PyTorch variants.

clusters.label: DDPM / NCSNPP diffusion image generation
clusters.notes: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPM) and score-based SDE (NCSNPP) models for unconditional image generation across multiple datasets and resolutions.

clusters.label: BERT small/medium encoder variants
clusters.notes: Grid of BERT encoder models varying depth (L) and hidden size (H) from the 'Well-Read Students Learn Better' paper; used as encoders and feature extractors.

clusters.label: MultiBERT pretraining reproducibility checkpoints
clusters.notes: Large suite of BERT models trained across multiple random seeds and captured at regular training step intervals for reproducibility and learning dynamics research.

clusters.label: ELECTRA encoder models
clusters.notes: ELECTRA discriminator and generator models for pretraining and fill-mask tasks; used as encoder backbones for downstream classification.

clusters.label: FNet encoder models
clusters.notes: Fourier-transform-based encoder models pretrained on C4; used as encoder backbones.

clusters.label: BigBird long-context encoders
clusters.notes: Long-context transformer models using sparse attention for QA, summarization, and pretraining tasks.

clusters.label: ByT5 byte-level models
clusters.notes: Byte-level T5 models operating on raw bytes for multilingual text generation without tokenization.

clusters.label: CANINE character-level encoders
clusters.notes: Tokenization-free character-level encoder models for multilingual feature extraction.

clusters.label: BERT2BERT translation models
clusters.notes: Encoder-decoder BERT models for sequence generation and machine translation (DE-EN, EN-DE).

clusters.label: MobileBERT compact BERT model
clusters.notes: Compact, task-agnostic BERT model optimized for mobile/edge deployment via knowledge distillation.

clusters.label: RemBERT multilingual encoder
clusters.notes: Multilingual BERT-style encoder supporting many languages for embeddings and classification.

clusters.label: MuRIL multilingual Indian languages BERT
clusters.notes: BERT-based encoder specialized for Indian languages, used for fill-mask and feature extraction.

clusters.label: MetricX translation quality estimation
clusters.notes: mT5-based translation quality estimation and reference-based metric models for MT evaluation in multiple sizes and quantized variants.

clusters.label: XTR dense retrieval models
clusters.notes: T5-based cross-token retrieval models for dense retrieval in English and multilingual variants.

Microsoft AI (MSR).summary: Microsoft Research maintains one of the broadest and most diverse AI model portfolios on HuggingFace, spanning general-purpose small language models (Phi series), domain-specialized LLMs (biomedical, code, math, scientific), and a vast array of accessory vision, audio, and embedding models — reflecting a research-driven strategy that prioritizes both foundational innovation and applied domain specialization.

clusters.label: Phi Small Language Model Family
clusters.notes: Microsoft's flagship small language model series spanning Phi-1 through Phi-4, including base, instruct, mini, MoE, reasoning, vision, and multimodal variants. Extensively quantized and exported to ONNX/GGUF formats for edge and web deployment.

clusters.label: Phi Vision and Multimodal Models
clusters.notes: Vision-language and multimodal extensions of the Phi family, supporting image understanding, audio/ASR, speech translation, OCR, and GUI grounding. Includes the Phi-Ground GUI agent and Phi-4 Reasoning Vision.

clusters.label: Phi Reasoning Models
clusters.notes: Phi-4 models fine-tuned for chain-of-thought reasoning, mathematics, and code, including compact mini variants and flash/fast inference versions.

clusters.label: General Conversational LLMs (DialoGPT, GODEL, UniLM)
clusters.notes: Open-domain dialogue and general-purpose language models including GPT-2-based DialoGPT, T5-based GODEL for grounded dialogue, and the unified UniLM series.

clusters.label: Reasoning and Distilled Research LLMs (Orca, MAI-DS-R1, FrogBoss)
clusters.notes: Research-focused general LLMs emphasizing reasoning via progressive learning (Orca-2), DeepSeek-R1 fine-tuning (MAI-DS-R1), and Qwen3-based reasoning models (FrogBoss/FrogMini).

clusters.label: BitNet Quantized Language Models
clusters.notes: 1-bit quantized general-purpose language models exploring extreme weight compression for efficient inference.

clusters.label: Biomedical NLP Models (BioGPT, BiomedBERT, BiomedELECTRA)
clusters.notes: GPT and BERT/ELECTRA-style models pre-trained on biomedical literature (PubMed) for text generation, fill-mask, and entity-level NLP tasks.

clusters.label: Medical Vision-Language Models (LLaVA-Med, MAIRA, MediPhi)
clusters.notes: Medical domain vision-language and language models for radiology report generation, clinical image understanding, and biomedical QA.

clusters.label: Biomedical Image Models (BiomedCLIP, BiomedVLP, BiomedParse)
clusters.notes: CLIP-style and encoder models for biomedical image classification, chest X-ray grounding, and biomedical image segmentation.

clusters.label: Protein Language Models (Dayhoff)
clusters.notes: Large family of Jamba-based protein language models at 170M and 3B scales trained on UniRef protein sequence databases, released at multiple training checkpoints.

clusters.label: Scientific Foundation Models (Aurora, ClimaX, MatterGen, Graphormer)
clusters.notes: Domain-specific foundation models for atmospheric/weather forecasting (Aurora, ClimaX), materials discovery (MatterGen), and molecular graph learning (Graphormer).

clusters.label: NatureLM Biology/Chemistry Models
clusters.notes: MoE-based models specialized for scientific reasoning in biology and chemistry domains.

clusters.label: Code Generation LLMs (CodeGPT, WaveCoder, NextCoder)
clusters.notes: GPT and LLaMA-based code generation models ranging from small GPT-2 fine-tunes to larger instruction-tuned coding assistants.

clusters.label: Code Encoder/Representation Models (CodeBERT, GraphCodeBERT, UniXcoder)
clusters.notes: RoBERTa-based encoder models for code feature extraction, masked language modeling, and cross-modal code representation.

clusters.label: Math and Reasoning Specialized Models (Rho, OptiMind)
clusters.notes: Models fine-tuned for mathematical reasoning and optimization problem solving, including code-interpreter variants.

clusters.label: Document Understanding Models (LayoutLM, MarkupLM, UDOP)
clusters.notes: Models combining text and visual layout signals for document, HTML/markup, and cross-modal document understanding tasks.

clusters.label: Florence-2 Vision Foundation Models
clusters.notes: Unified vision foundation models supporting captioning, detection, segmentation, and VQA in base and large sizes.

clusters.label: GIT Image-to-Text Models
clusters.notes: Generative Image-to-Text Transformer models fine-tuned on vision-language benchmarks including COCO, TextCaps, VQA, and video captioning.

clusters.label: Kosmos Multimodal Models
clusters.notes: Multimodal grounding and OCR-focused vision-language models for image understanding and document reading.

clusters.label: GUI Agent Models (OmniParser, GUI-Actor, Phi-Ground)
clusters.notes: Models for GUI screenshot parsing, grounding, and computer-use agent tasks built on Qwen2-VL and Phi-3.5-vision backbones.

clusters.label: Robotics Vision-Language-Action Models (Magma, VILLA-X, VITRA-VLA)
clusters.notes: Multimodal models targeting robotic manipulation, spatial reasoning, and vision-language-action tasks.

clusters.label: 3D and Video Generation Models (TRELLIS, VQ-Diffusion, Reducio VAE)
clusters.notes: Generative models for image-to-3D, text-to-3D, and vector-quantized image/video generation.

clusters.label: Speech Models (SpeechT5, UniSpeech, WavLM, VibeVoice)
clusters.notes: Comprehensive speech model suite covering ASR, TTS, voice conversion, speaker diarization/verification, and self-supervised speech representation learning.

clusters.label: DeBERTa Encoder Models
clusters.notes: DeBERTa encoder family across v1/v2/v3 generations in multiple sizes, including multilingual variants and MNLI-finetuned classifiers.

clusters.label: MiniLM Distilled Sentence Models
clusters.notes: Compact distilled BERT-style models for efficient text classification and embedding tasks.

clusters.label: Harrier Multilingual Embedding Models
clusters.notes: Multilingual sentence embedding models of varying sizes based on Qwen3 and Gemma3 backbones.

clusters.label: LLM2CLIP and BiomedCLIP Contrastive Models
clusters.notes: CLIP-style contrastive models enhanced with LLM text encoders for improved zero-shot image-text alignment, including biomedical variants.

clusters.label: BEiT / FocalNet / Swin / SwinV2 / ResNet Vision Classifiers
clusters.notes: Broad family of vision transformer and CNN image classification/segmentation models spanning BEiT, FocalNet, Swin, SwinV2, and ResNet architectures at various scales.

clusters.label: Object Detection Models (Conditional DETR, Table Transformer)
clusters.notes: Transformer-based object detection models for general detection and specialized table detection/structure recognition in documents.

clusters.label: OCR Models (TrOCR, DiT)
clusters.notes: Vision-encoder-decoder models for optical character recognition of handwritten, printed, and scene text, plus document image classification.

clusters.label: Medical Image Encoders (RAD-DINO, RadEdit, CXR-BERT)
clusters.notes: DINOv2-based radiology image encoders, diffusion-based medical image editing, and chest X-ray vision-language feature extractors.

clusters.label: Geospatial and Satellite Models (SatCLIP)
clusters.notes: CLIP-style geospatial location encoders trained on satellite imagery at multiple resolutions.

clusters.label: Multilingual and Cross-Lingual Encoders (XLM-Align, InfoXLM, mDeBERTa)
clusters.notes: Cross-lingual alignment and representation models for multilingual fill-mask, feature extraction, and NLU tasks.

clusters.label: Multilingual Generation Models (XProphetNet, ProphetNet)
clusters.notes: ProphetNet seq2seq models for summarization and question generation, with multilingual XGlue fine-tuned variants.

clusters.label: Distilled and Compressed BERT Models (XtremDistil, MPNet)
clusters.notes: Task-agnostic distilled BERT models and MPNet encoders optimized for efficient text classification and feature extraction.

clusters.label: Table and Structured Data Models (TAPEX, MarkupLM)
clusters.notes: BART-based models for table question answering and fact verification via SQL execution simulation.

clusters.label: Video and Audio Accessory Models (X-CLIP, VidTok, MSCLAP)
clusters.notes: Video-language contrastive models for zero-shot video classification, video tokenization VAEs, and contrastive audio-language pretraining.

clusters.label: Prompt Compression and Optimization (LLMLingua, Promptist)
clusters.notes: Token classifiers for prompt compression/filtering in LLM pipelines, and a GPT-2 model for prompt optimization.

clusters.label: Dialogue Response Ranking (DialogRPT)
clusters.notes: GPT-2 based dialogue response ranking/scoring models for evaluating conversational response quality along multiple dimensions.

clusters.label: ONNX/WebNN Optimized Deployment Variants
clusters.notes: ONNX and WebNN-exported variants of various models (Phi, Whisper, Stable Diffusion, SAM) optimized for browser, edge, and CPU/DirectML deployment.

clusters.label: Miscellaneous Research Accessories (RenderFormer, BLOOM DeepSpeed, UniHanLM)
clusters.notes: Assorted research accessory models including neural rendering, DeepSpeed-optimized BLOOM inference variants, and CJK cross-lingual encoders.

NVIDIA Research.summary: NVIDIA Research maintains one of the broadest and most technically diverse model portfolios in the industry, spanning general-purpose and specialized LLMs (Nemotron, AceMath, reasoning), multimodal vision-language models, world-generation and robotics models (Cosmos, GR00T), scientific/biomedical AI, and a large collection of accessory models covering speech/ASR, vision encoders, embeddings, safety classifiers, and quantized inference variants — reflecting NVIDIA's dual role as a hardware company and full-stack AI research lab.

clusters.label: Nemotron General-Purpose LLMs
clusters.notes: Core Nemotron family spanning 3B–340B parameters, including base, instruct, RLHF/SteerLM, hybrid SSM-attention (Nemotron-H), MoE (Super/Nano), ultra-long-context, and flash/elastic variants. Covers the full Nemotron-3, Nemotron-4, and Nemotron-H generations.

clusters.label: Nemotron Quantized & Distilled Variants
clusters.notes: Efficiency-optimized derivatives of Nemotron and Llama/Mistral base models via structured pruning, knowledge distillation, FP8/NVFP4/INT4 quantization, and ONNX export. Includes Minitron depth/width-pruned distillations and KV-cache compressed variants.

clusters.label: Quantized Third-Party LLMs (ModelOpt)
clusters.notes: NVIDIA ModelOpt-quantized versions of third-party models (DeepSeek, Qwen2.5/3, Llama 3.1/4, Gemma, GLM, Kimi, Phi, MiniMax) using FP8, NVFP4, or INT4 precision, often paired with Eagle3 speculative decoding drafts.

clusters.label: AceInstruct / AceMath / AceReason Reasoning Series
clusters.notes: Math and reasoning specialist LLMs built on Qwen2 backbones, including chain-of-thought training, reinforcement learning (RLVR), and paired reward models. AceInstruct covers general instruction tuning; AceMath targets math; AceReason targets math+code reasoning.

clusters.label: OpenMath / OpenReasoning / OpenCodeReasoning Series
clusters.notes: Open dataset-trained math and code reasoning fine-tunes of Llama, CodeLlama, Mistral, and Nemotron backbones using OpenMathInstruct-1/2, OpenMathReasoning, and OpenCodeReasoning datasets.

clusters.label: Nemotron Reward, Feedback & Alignment Models
clusters.notes: Reward models, generative reward models (GenRM), BRRM, feedback/edit/select models, and DPO/PPO/SteerLM aligned chat models for RLHF data curation pipelines. Trained on HelpSteer2/3 datasets.

clusters.label: Nemotron Code & Agent Models
clusters.notes: Code agent models for terminal/CLI tasks and multi-agent orchestration at 8B–32B scales.

clusters.label: ChatQA Conversational QA Models
clusters.notes: Specialized conversational QA models optimized for retrieval-augmented generation and long-context dialogue.

clusters.label: Content Safety & Guardrail Models
clusters.notes: Safety classifiers and guardrail models for content moderation, jailbreak detection, PII/PHI detection, and topic control. Includes Aegis (LlamaGuard-based), NemoGuard, Nemotron Safety Guard, and GLiNER PII detection.

clusters.label: Cosmos World Generation Models
clusters.notes: Physical-AI world simulation models across Predict1/2/2.5 and Transfer generations, using both autoregressive and diffusion architectures for text-to-video, image-to-video, video-to-world, and AV multiview synthesis. Includes surgical and diffusion renderer variants.

clusters.label: Cosmos Tokenizers & Embeddings
clusters.notes: Continuous and discrete image/video tokenizers (CI/DI/CV/DV), video embedding models, and a surgical image tokenizer/autoencoder supporting the Cosmos world-generation ecosystem.

clusters.label: Cosmos Policy & Reasoning (Robotics/Physical AI)
clusters.notes: World-model-based robotics policy models (ALOHA, LIBERO, RoboCasa datasets) and multimodal reasoning VLMs (Cosmos-Reason built on Qwen2.5-VL/Qwen3-VL) for physical-world reasoning and autonomous vehicle applications.

clusters.label: GR00T Humanoid Robot Foundation Models
clusters.notes: NVIDIA's GR00T series of humanoid robot foundation models and task-specific fine-tunes across multiple embodiments (ALOHA, SO-ARM, Franka) and datasets. Includes Cosmos-Dreams world model integration.

clusters.label: GEAR / Kimodo Robotics Controllers & Motion Capture
clusters.notes: Whole-body control and RL models for humanoid robotics (GEAR) and motion capture/body model retargeting for robot embodiments (Kimodo).

clusters.label: Multimodal Vision-Language Models (VILA, Eagle, NVLM, DAM)
clusters.notes: General-purpose vision-language models spanning Eagle2/2.5, NVLM-D, VILA-HD, VideoITG, DAM (region-level description), and nvOmni omni-modal variants. Covers image, video, and document understanding.

clusters.label: Nemotron OCR & Document Understanding
clusters.notes: OCR and document layout analysis VLMs for text extraction, document parsing, and element detection from PDFs and images.

clusters.label: EGM Embodied Grounding Models
clusters.notes: Visual grounding models trained with GRPO reinforcement learning and SFT on Qwen3-VL backbone for embodied AI tasks.

clusters.label: C-RADIO / RADIO / PS3 Vision Encoders
clusters.notes: Multi-resolution vision foundation models for image feature extraction used as backbones in VLM and downstream vision pipelines. Includes RADIO, C-RADIO, and PS3 progressive-scaling variants.

clusters.label: QLIP Image Tokenizers & FasterViT / GCViT Encoders
clusters.notes: Quantized CLIP-based image tokenizers (QLIP), efficient vision transformers (FasterViT), and global context vision transformers (GCViT) for image classification and encoding.

clusters.label: MambaVision Image Classification & Segmentation
clusters.notes: Mamba-based hybrid vision backbone models for image classification (ImageNet-1K/21K) and semantic segmentation (ADE20K), at various scales.

clusters.label: SegFormer / MIT Vision Segmentation Models
clusters.notes: Mix Transformer (MiT) encoder backbones and SegFormer models fine-tuned on ADE20K and Cityscapes for semantic segmentation.

clusters.label: NV Embedding & Retrieval Models
